overview: Oops! All Spells is a Vintage control archetype that leverages instant-speed spellcasting and powerful counters to dictate the pace of the game. By combining cheap mana acceleration with versatile interaction, the deck aims to stabilize early, outdraw opponents, and close games with efficient threats or recursive value engines. The W/U/B color identity provides a robust suite of removal, draw, and countermagic tailored to the Vintage metagame.
win_conditions: Stabilizing with Pact of Negation to protect a game-ending threat
Outdrawing opponents with efficient card advantage engines
Using Beseech the Mirror to generate infinite value or lethal damage
Deploying resilient creatures that survive interaction to attack directly

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two lands and a cheap interaction spell
Combinations featuring Chrome Mox and a one-drop counter or draw spell
Packages that can answer turn-one threats or establish early tempo
mulligan_criteria: Hands with only high-cost threats and no answers
Draws missing at least two sources of instant-speed spells

early_game: Prioritize playing lands and Chrome Mox to enable turn-one or turn-two interaction
Use Pact of Negation or other counters to disrupt opponent's key plays
Hold up mana to respond to threats rather than committing resources prematurely
mid_game: Stabilize the board by removing problematic permanents and drawing into more answers
Leverage Beseech the Mirror to copy and replay spells for massive value or board control
Begin deploying resilient threats once the opponent's interaction is exhausted
late_game: Transition to a grindy attrition battle using superior card advantage and recursion
Protect your win conditions with layered countermagic and damage prevention
Close out the game by attacking with untapped creatures or resolving uncounterable finishers

key_cards.name: Chrome Mox
key_cards.role: Zero-mana artifact that taps to add one mana of any color
key_cards.notes: Play on turn one to enable early interaction or fetch lands; prioritize keeping it in hands with cheap spells.

key_cards.name: Beseech the Mirror
key_cards.role: Sorcery that copies target instant or sorcery spell you control and lets you cast the copy
key_cards.notes: Use to double up on countermagic, draw spells, or finishers; sequence after resolving the original to maximize impact.

key_cards.name: Pact of Negation
key_cards.role: Enchantment that counters target spell and prevents all damage for one turn
key_cards.notes: Hold up three mana to cast; best used to protect key threats or negate opponent's game-winning combos.

sequencing_tips: Always hold up Pact of Negation's mana requirement before committing to other spells
Cast Beseech the Mirror after your primary spell resolves to ensure the copy targets the correct state
Use Chrome Mox to fix mana for counterspells rather than accelerating threats early
Prioritize countering the opponent's most impactful spell over their mana sources

matchups.advice: Focus on early disruption and holding up Pact of Negation for their key pieces
Use damage prevention and countermagic to stall until you can deploy resilient threats
Race their resource count by using Beseech the Mirror to outvalue their interaction suite
